Section Title: Critical Minerals: Energy > Rule Investment Newsletter #9 > Uranium
Content:
Around 150Mlb of yellowcake was produced in 2024, but 190Mlb was consumed, which is eating into inventories. The US Energy Information Administration (EIA) warns of a uranium shortfall over the next 10 years as mine supply fails to keep pace with expected increases in demand. It sees a 50Mlb deficit in 2025 growing. The *Uranium Market Outlook* projected the production gap at ~51Mlb through 2026, ~355Mlb through 2035, 890Mlb through 2040, and 1.75Blb through 2045. Uranium prices have risen to about $80/lb on the back of shortfalls in production from Cameco in Canada, and Kazatomprom in Kazakhstan, and pricing is still not high enough to stimulate new capacity construction. US utilities are the world’s largest consumers of uranium, with a current demand of 47Mlb/y. There are 439 nuclear power plants in operation globally with 70 under construction and 108 planned. Builds are at a 25-year high. Here are Rick’s thoughts:
 ... 
Section Title: Companies > Arizona Sonoran Copper (TSX:ASCU/OTC:ASCUF) > Cameco (NYSE:CCJ/TSX:CCO)
Content:
Share price change YoY 75%
Rick believes the surest way to make sure money in uranium is to buy Cameco. Cameco produced 10.6Mlb of uranium in the first six months of 2025. In October, together with Westinghouse Electric and Brookfield Asset Management, it announced that the US government entered into a strategic partnership to accelerate the deployment of nuclear power that will include at least $80B of new reactors to be built across the US. Brookfield and Cameco formed a strategic partnership in October 2022 to acquire Westinghouse. Rick says:
Cameco may fall 35%, but it’s the bellwether for uranium, and uranium is a 15-year no-brainer.

results.title: Rick Rule 2026: Commodities, Gold & Energy | 2026-01-14 | Investing News | Stockhouse
excerpts:
Section Title: Rick Rule 2026: Commodities, Gold & Energy
Content:
As global markets move deeper into 2026, few investors have navigated commodity cycles with as much consistency and candor as [Rick Rule. President and CEO of Rule Investment Media,](https://www.ruleinvestmentmedia.com/) Rule has spent decades analyzing capital flows, resource scarcity and the behavioural traps that ensnare investors when cycles turn.
In this Expert Exchange interview with host Coreena Robertson, Rule shares his outlook on commodities, precious metals, energy markets and why Canada remains one of the most overlooked jurisdictions for disciplined resource investors.
 ... 
Section Title: Rick Rule 2026: Commodities, Gold & Energy > Oil & Gas: Still Essential, Still Mispriced
Content:
“The best sector I see is oil and gas,” Rule says. “I told you before, I love hate, and I do. Oil and gas are hated.”
Despite years of divestment pressure and political headwinds, Rule made it clear that oil and gas remain central to the global economy. In addition, it is also attrractie from an investment standpoint.
“I like oil and gas,” Rule said plainly. “Not because it’s fashionable, but because it’s necessary.”
He noted that years of underinvestment has constrained supply just as global demand continues to rise, particularly outside of North America and Europe.
“You can talk about electrification all you want, but hydrocarbons still underpin modern life.”he added. “The world doesn’t run on press releases – it runs on energy.”
*Rick Rule*
 ... 
Section Title: Rick Rule 2026: Commodities, Gold & Energy > Copper, Electrification & Reality
Content:
Copper remains a prime example of the tension between political ambition and physical reality. “Everybody agrees copper is essential,” Rule said. “The problem is that mines don’t get built on timelines that match policy goals.”
While government incentives may help at the margins, Rule stresses that permitting, soical opposition, and long development timelines remain formidable obstacles.
 ... 
Section Title: Rick Rule 2026: Commodities, Gold & Energy > Want more learning opportunities in 2026?
Content:
For more Rick Rule, check out Rick’s previous segment on the economy, critical minerals, [“We’re in a New Era”](https://themarketonline.ca/rick-rule-on-the-economy-critical-minerals-battle-bank-were-in-a-new-era-2025-07-31/) as well as his [advice on investment reads](https://stockhouse.com/news/newswire/2024/08/14/book-some-time-for-rick-rule-s-summertime-investment-reads) and [Wise advice from ‘The Intelligent Investor](https://themarketonline.ca/rick-rule-wise-advice-from-the-intelligent-investor-2025-03-27/) ‘.
Be sure to stay up to date on all the latest stock market news at [Stockhouse.com](https://stockhouse.com/) .
**Join the discussion:** Find out what everybody’s saying about uranium, oil and gas, precious metal stocks and more by checking out [Stockhouse’s stock forums and message boards](https://stockhouse.com/community/bullboards) .

Rick Rule's Portfolio + Gold, Silver, Uranium & Copper Stock Picks Exposed, MUST WATCH🚨
December 17, 2025
In this exclusive interview, legendary natural resources investor Rick Rule shares his bold 2026 outlook on commodities, declaring the gold bull market still in its early-to-mid stages while a broader supercycle looms for industrial metals like copper and oil. With gold prices soaring past $2,700 and silver outperforming dramatically, Rick reveals his current portfolio allocation—around 40% in cash and liquids, shifting heavily into undervalued oil & gas, and selective gold equities—and dives deep into top mining stocks including GoldMining Inc., Royal Gold, McEwen Mining, Hot Chili, Chalice Mining, and more. He discusses the coming M&A explosion in junior miners, why many are historically undervalued, the revolutionary role of AI in resource investing, and essential advice for young investors looking to outperform in the sector.
 ...

Section Title: ... > Comments 218
Content:
1.3K Likes 38,822 Views Feb 16 2026 Legendary investor Rick Rule delivers a bold 2026 outlook on commodities, warning of an epic opportunity in the oil and gas sector due to massive global underinvestment in sustaining capex—predicting oil prices could surge to $85–$90 per barrel in the next 3–4 years amid structural supply deficits. He reveals his portfolio shifts: selling a significant portion of physical silver after its run-up to redeploy into leveraged silver equities, quality oil names like Exxon, Chevron, and high-yielding Canadian producers (Suncor, Canadian Natural, and more), plus plans to get more aggressive on speculative juniors in the last unloved sector—conventional offshore and frontier exploration in emerging markets, where one success could deliver 20x returns.
Section Title: ... > Comments 218
Content:
Rick also covers the ongoing gold bull market as a long-term purchasing power hedge (with USD potentially losing 75% value over a decade), copper as the next major bull play from chronic deficits and electrification demand, and his steady stance on uranium via Cameco and physical holdings. Don't miss this must-watch deep dive into contrarian natural resource investing, macro warnings, and Rick's picks for profiting from hated sectors—subscribe for more expert commodity insights!

results.title: 2025 review, 2026 preview - Rick Rule | Substack
excerpts:
Section Title: 2025 review, 2026 preview > RIN #10
Content:
Dec 18, 2025
143
9
Share
[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!cbu0!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F7118afb6-e198-471b-a249-59d3e9a83072_1024x1024.png)
As 2025 draws to a close, it is time to look back on the first year of the Rule Investment Newsletter and some of the themes we have discussed, and to look ahead to what we can expect in 2026. Overall, we believe we have hit quite a few nails squarely on the head this year, and we hope that you and your investment decisions have benefited from that.
We hope you find the Rule Investment Newsletter useful and profitable. If so, we would like to ask you to forward this to a friend, allowing them to sign up to receive it as well.
[RIN posts list](https://realrickrule.substack.com/profile/posts)
 ... 
Section Title: 2025 review, 2026 preview > RIN #10 > Financings
Content:
Rick sold 25% of his juniors in October; by so doing, he recouped all the capital he had invested since 2020, and he ploughed those funds back into higher quality names such as **Franco Nevada** , **Wheaton Precious Metals** , and into the oil and gas space. He explained it thus:
“I kept the upside and eliminated all the downside, while going up the quality trail.”
 ... 
Section Title: 2025 review, 2026 preview > RIN #10 > Copper
Content:
Copper has also been at the forefront of Trump’s energy narrative. It was the subject of a s232 investigation this year, which resulted in tariffs on semi-finished copper producers and moves to cease exports of copper scrap (to China). This has benefited US project developers, particularly those who seek to produce copper cathodes, which can be transformed into tubes and cables, such as 2025 Rule Symposium participants **Arizona Sonoran Copper** and **Ivanhoe Electric** . Overt political support combined with an intent to reduce permitting times, which have been multi-decade, has been on the copper sector’s wish list for many years. An example of the impact of this is the **BHP** - **Rio Tinto** Resolution joint venture in Arizona, which has finally been permitted and has largely navigated an almost endless stream of legal challenges.
 ... 
Section Title: 2025 review, 2026 preview > RIN #10 > Copper
Content:
An emerging copper growth market is AI and datacentres. Wood Mackenzie picked this as one of the resource themes of 2025, which also has significant implications for power generation, and by extension, nuclear power and uranium. It could also advance the case for small modular reactors, given that data centre operating costs are about half power-related. Wood Mackenzie said:
“The US power market, a low-growth zone for decades, is forecast to see its AI-driven power demand grow at a massive compound rate of 20% to 2030. There is a growing call on gas-fired power, but with rising gas prices and the rapid inflation of build costs for new power plants power prices are expected to rise.”
 ... 
Section Title: 2025 review, 2026 preview > RIN #10 > Energy
Content:
The oil and gas industry has faced a challenging market in 2025. As the year comes to a close, the oil price is hovering around the $60/barrel mark, considerably below the $100-120/barrel it has been. We featured the [Canadian oil and gas sector in our first issue, RIN #1](https://realrickrule.substack.com/p/canadian-oil-and-gas) , and the stocks we discussed have held their ground despite challenging market conditions and are poised for a rebound as the market and pricing improve. Importantly for investors, the majority of these companies have continued to pay healthy dividends, yielding 4-7% at a time when commodity prices have been relatively stable. Imagine what they may do as prices rise. Here is Rick:
“Assuming I’m early, which I invariably am, the Canadian names give you a 6% basic yield, so the time value of money objection is obviated by the dividend.”
Section Title: 2025 review, 2026 preview > RIN #10 > Energy
Content:
[RIN #1 Canadian Oil & Gas](https://realrickrule.substack.com/p/canadian-oil-and-gas)
In closing, many thanks for accompanying RIN in 2025. We aim to bring you many more investment theme insights in 2026. Until then, happy holidays.
